subsection.heading: Jurisdiction
subsection.chapeau: The district courts of the United States shall have jurisdiction over civil actions to—

paragraph.content: restrain any violation of section 6726 or 6745 of this title; and
compel the taking of any action required by or under this chapter or the Convention.

paragraph.heading: In general
paragraph.chapeau: A civil action described in subsection (a) may be brought—

subparagraph.content: in the case of a civil action described in subsection (a)(1), in the United States district court for the judicial district in which any act, omission, or transaction constituting a violation of section 6726 or 6745 of this title occurred or in which the defendant is found or transacts business; or
in the case of a civil action described in subsection (a)(2), in the United States district court for the judicial district in which the defendant is found or transacts business.

content.p: In any such civil action process may be served on a defendant wherever the defendant may reside or may be found, whether the defendant resides or may be found within the United States or elsewhere.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: This chapter, referred to in subsec. (a)(2), was in the original “this Act” and was translated as reading “this division”, meaning div. I of Pub. L. 105–277, Oct. 21, 1998, 112 Stat. 2681–856, known as the Chemical Weapons Convention Implementation Act of 1998, to reflect the probable intent of Congress. For complete classification of division I to the Code, see Short Title note set out under section 6701 of this title and Tables.
